channel.title: Visual Studio Code on Tue Nguyen
channel.description: Recent content in Visual Studio Code on Tue Nguyen

item.title: Code formatting
item.description: Step 1: Install the Prettier extension. Step 2: Set Prettier as the default formatter in settings.json (you can do this via GUI). { "editor.defaultFormatter": "esbenp.prettier-vscode" } Step 3: Set other recommended options in settings.json. { "editor.formatOnSave": true }

item.title: Cool features
item.description: Zen mode Zen mode is distract-free mode that hides away unnecessary detail and allows you focus just on the code. The zen mode can be toggled using command palette. Open command palette: Ctrl + Shift + P Type zen mode and hit Enter Minimap For a large file with many lines of code, you can quickly scroll up and down the file using the minimap on the right side of the editor.

item.title: Interface
item.description: Editor window The editor window is the main part of the editor where you write and edit your code. It is at the center of the IDE with largest estate. If you open multiple files, they will appears as tabs. You can switching between tab using Ctrl + Tab (forward) or Ctrl + Shift + Tab (backward). You can use the split mode to see multiple files simultaneously. In this mode, the window can be split vertically (side by side), horizontally (top and bottom), or a mixture of these two.

item.title: Introduction
item.description: What is VS Code? Visual Studio Code (or VS Code) is a free, open-source, lightweight, and cross-platform code editor developed by Microsoft based on the Electron Framework. It is one of the most popular code editors used and recommended by programmers at all levels, from beginners to experienced professionals. Features Here are some of the highlighted features that have made VS Code popular. Free. You pay nothing to use it. Lightweight.

item.title: Keyboard shortcuts
item.description: Navigation Go to settings: Ctrl + , Go to explorer: Ctrl + Shift + E Go to version control: Ctrl + Shift + G Go to debugger: Ctrl + Shift + D Go to folder find and replace: Ctrl + Shift + F Go to extensions: Ctrl + Shift + X Find and replace In the whole folder Find: Ctrl + Shift + F Replace: click on > of the search box to show the replace box In the current file Find: Ctrl + F Replace: click on > of the search box to show the replace box Command palette Go to command palette: Ctrl + Shift + P Working with multiple files Switch between tabs Forward: Ctrl + Tab Backward: Ctrl + Shift + Tab Search file by name: Ctrl + P Working with one file Go to line in the current file: Ctrl + G Close the file: Ctrl + W Terminal Toggle terminal: Ctrl + ~ Other Copy a line: put the cursor at the line and press Ctrl + C Select a word: put the cursor at the word and press Ctrl + D

item.title: Personalization
item.description: This is my preferred settings for VS Code. Editor Editor font size: 18 px Word wrap: on Word wrap column: 80 characters Theme Best choice: One Dark Pro Flat Next best choices: One Dark Pro, Visual Studio Dark Terminal To be updated
